assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements applying IFRS 9 hedge accounting.
The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intercompany receivable/payable, ie as a recognised monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components thereof.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
